context: Tests whether the agent avoids eval when performing dynamic substitutions with user-supplied config values, correctly quotes variables throughout (especially multi-word values), and uses safe alternatives like sed or envsubst for template rendering.

checklist.name: No eval used
checklist.description: Script does NOT use `eval` anywhere in the substitution or rendering logic

checklist.name: Safe substitution method
checklist.description: Script uses sed, envsubst, awk, or another safe method for placeholder substitution (not eval or source-based variable injection)

checklist.name: Multi-word values handled
checklist.description: Substitution correctly handles config values containing spaces (e.g. "Q1 2026 Revenue Report") without breaking the output

checklist.name: Quoted variable expansions
checklist.description: Variables derived from config values are quoted when passed to commands ("${value}" not $value)

checklist.name: Correct output produced
checklist.description: Running the script with inputs/template.txt and inputs/config.cfg produces output where all four {{KEY}} placeholders are replaced with the correct values from config.cfg

checklist.name: Input validation
checklist.description: Script validates that the template file and config file both exist before processing

checklist.name: readonly or local constants
checklist.description: Script uses `readonly` for at least one script-level constant or `local` for function-scoped variables

checklist.name: Logging or status messages
checklist.description: Script emits at least one status message (to stderr or stdout) indicating progress or completion
